audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Judean advantage, Divine faithfulness, universal accountability, Law's function, righteousness/justice through trust, grace, redemption, hilasterion, boasting excluded.

verses.restored: What then is the advantage of the Judean, or what is the profit of circumcision?
Much in every way. First indeed, that they were entrusted with the oracles of the Divine.
For what if some were untrusting? Will their untrust nullify the trustworthiness of the Divine?
May it not be. Let the Divine be true though every human be false, just as it is written: So that you may be made righteous/just in your words and prevail when you are judged.
But if our injustice commends the righteousness/justice of the Divine, what shall we say? Is the Divine unjust to bring wrath? I speak according to human reasoning.
May it not be. Otherwise how will the Divine judge the world?
But if the truth of the Divine abounded in my falsehood to his glory, why am I still judged as sinner/misaligned?
And why not say, as we are slandered and as some claim we say, Let us do harmful things so good may come? Their judgment is just.
What then? Are we better off? Not entirely; for we have already charged that both Judeans and Greeks are all under sin/misalignment,
just as it is written: There is none righteous/just, not even one;
there is none understanding; there is none seeking out the Divine.
All turned aside; together they became useless; there is none doing kindness, there is not even one.
Their throat is an opened grave; with their tongues they deceived; venom of asps is under their lips.
Their mouth is full of curse and bitterness.
Their feet are swift to pour out blood;
ruin and misery are in their ways,
and the way of peace they did not know.
There is no reverence of the Divine before their eyes.
Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those in the Law, so that every mouth may be stopped and the whole world may become accountable to the Divine.
Because from works of Law no flesh will be made righteous/just before him; for through Law comes knowledge of sin/misalignment.
But now, apart from Law, righteousness/justice of the Divine has been manifested, being witnessed by the Law and the Prophets,
righteousness/justice of the Divine through trust of Jesus Messiah for all who trust. For there is no distinction,
for all sinned/misaligned and fall short of the glory of the Divine,
being made righteous/just freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Messiah Jesus,

whom the Divine set forth as mercy-seat/atoning sign through trust in his blood, for showing his righteousness/justice because of the passing over of former sins/misalignments in the restraint of the Divine,
for showing his righteousness/justice in the present time, so that he might be just and the one making righteous/just the one from the trust of Jesus.
Where then is boasting? It is excluded. Through what law? Of works? No, but through law of trust.
For we calculate that a human is made righteous/just by trust apart from works of Law.
Or is the Divine of Judeans only? Is he not also of nations? Yes, also of nations,
since the Divine is one, who will make righteous/just circumcision from trust and uncircumcision through trust.
Do we then nullify Law through trust? May it not be; rather, we establish Law.

translation_notes: The phrase in 3:22 and 3:26 may be heard as trust in Jesus Messiah and/or the trust/faithfulness of Jesus Messiah. The restored wording preserves the genitive ambiguity where possible.
Hilasterion in 3:25 is rendered mercy-seat/atoning sign to avoid flattening complex temple imagery into a single later theory.
